meta_description: Custom injection molding service, a wide range of capabilities and 5000+ materials. Prototype and production tooling. SPI & VDI finish.

sections.heading: Online injection molding service
sections.text: Online injection molding service Access a wide range of custom services from our specialized network of injection molding service companies. Prototype and production tooling. Upload your parts for a free quote and expert feedback. Get an injection molding quote All uploads are secure and confidential

sections.heading: Our custom injection molding servicesÂ
sections.text: Our custom injection molding servicesÂ Create parts of all complexities sized from 3 mm (0.12 in) to 2 m (79 in) with Protolabs Network (formerly Hubs). Lead times are as fast as 10 days, and we offer local or global production options. Our manufacturing partners typically use 20 to 3,000 ton injection machines, depending on part size, complexity, and volume. DIN 16901-130 and tighter tolerances are available on request. Plastic injection molding Injection molding is the manufacturing process of injecting high-pressure thermoplastic into a mold. Ideal for high-volume production. Learn more Insert molding A manufacturing process in which a part is molded around a pre-installed insert in the...

sections.heading: Tooling - prototyping vs production
sections.text: Tooling - prototyping vs production Prototyping Ideal for rapid prototyping and low-volume production Tool shot life â 1k to 100k units Fast turnaround and cost-efficient construction Aluminum or steel Production Longer-lasting tooling for larger volumes Tool shot life â 100k to 1M units Automated production for cost-efficiency at higher volumes Hardened steel for longer life and a scalable unit price

sections.heading: Secondary operations
sections.text: Secondary operations We offer several secondary operations such as painting or heat tapping inserts. You can specify which secondary operations are needed in the âcustom requirementsâ part of your quote request. Here are some examples of secondary operations we provide: Inserts Pad Printing Painting Silk screening Ultrasonic welding Laser engraving UV coating Heat staking Hot stamping Post-mold machining

sections.heading: Injection molding surface finishes
sections.text: Injection molding surface finishes SPI surface finishes We offer any type of SPI surface finish, such as A-1, D-2 or D-3. VDI surface finishes We offer any type of VDI surface finish, such as VDI28, VDI30 or VDI 33. Find the right SPI finish for your part

sections.heading: Injection molding materials
sections.text: Injection molding materials Injection molding uses a huge range of plastics, making it ideal for many applications across nearly every industry. We can source thousands of materials on request. Please contact networksales@protolabs.com for specific material requests. Explore our injection molding materials

sections.heading: The process of our injection molding service
sections.text: The process of our injection molding service 1. Upload your design Upload your parts to our platform to have your project reviewed by our team 2. Receive a quote Your account manager will work with our technical team and manufacturing partners to send you a quote within 48 hours 3. DFM & tool approval Receive extensive Design For Manufacturing analysis to approve before construction of your tool begins 4. Receive T1 samples We send you a golden sample for approval before starting production 5. Production & delivery We produce your parts to match the golden sample & deliver them to you 6. Reorder as required You can easily reorder parts by contacting your account manager

sections.heading: The injection molding timeline
sections.text: The injection molding timeline Ready to start the injection molding process? Download our Gantt chart to help keep your injection molding project on schedule, within budget and to spec.

sections.heading: The Protolabs Network Standard - consistent quality, every time
sections.text: The Protolabs Network Standard - consistent quality, every time Protolabs Network applies a rigorous set of standards to every custom part, ensuring quality and consistency for injection molded components. We inspect each order dimensionally and visually during and after production. Tolerances & dimensional accuracy Tolerances & dimensional accuracy Learn more Mold finish standards Mold finish standards Learn more Quality control procedures Quality control procedures Learn more Read more about The Protolabs Network Standard

sections.text: What are the customer experiences of our injection molding service? "It was really helpful to have someone to spar with and test out new ideas on the platform, get a new price for them, and ultimately go into production.â Otect âHaving someone who can be in touch with the manufacturers was essential for us.â LEKTRI.CO âYou can just upload the parts and get a quote directly. It was just awesomeâ. Zinstax

sections.heading: The advantages of injection molding with us Â
sections.text: The advantages of injection molding with us Â Variety of materials Injection molding offers vast flexibility in material selection. Depending on the mechanical properties your parts require, you can achieve impact- and chemical-resistance, rubber-like elasticity and dimensional stability with molded parts. Our experts can help to manufacture molded parts that are optimized to perform in industrial applications, including parts designed for aerospace, automotive and consumer electronics. We can source thousands of custom materials on request and can advise you on the best choice for your project while minimizing your costs along the way.Â Â Single, multi-cavity, and family molds No matter th...

sections.heading: FAQ's about injection molding service
sections.text: FAQ's about injection molding service How much does plastic injection molding cost? There are many factors that determine the cost of injection molding your custom parts. The three main expenses are tooling, materials and production. Find out how to reduce your manufacturing costs in our injection molding guide . Who owns the injection mold? Customers retain ownership of all custom tooling they develop, however, any reusable proprietary components developed by the manufacturing partner (MP) will become the MPâs property. All custom tooling will remain at the MPâs facility for a minimum of two years unless otherwise agreed. If the tooling is inactive after two years, the MP may contact P...
